q.en: Who wrote The Red Badge of Courage?

options.en: Louisa May Alcott, a writer of family stories
options.zh: 路易莎·梅·奥尔科特，写过家庭故事

options.en: Stephen Crane
options.zh: 斯蒂芬·克莱恩

options.en: Mark Twain, famous for river tales
options.zh: 马克·吐温，以河流故事闻名

options.en: Herman Melville, who wrote sea adventures
options.zh: 赫尔曼·梅尔维尔，写过航海冒险故事

explain.en: The book says it was adapted from the writings of Stephen Crane, who wrote it in 1895.
explain.zh: 书中说明本作改编自斯蒂芬·克莱恩的作品，他于1895年写成此书。

q.en: After his first battle in Chapter 6, how does Henry feel at first?
q.zh: 在第6章打完第一仗后，亨利一开始感觉如何？

options.en: He is sad because the fighting has stopped
options.zh: 他很伤心，因为战斗停下了

options.en: He is scared and wishes to go home right away
options.zh: 他很害怕，想立刻回家

options.en: He feels proud and is full of self-satisfaction
options.zh: 他感到自豪，充满自我满足

options.en: He is angry at his friends for no real reason according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他无缘无故对朋友们发火（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Henry wakes up happy and goes into an ecstasy of self-satisfaction, thinking he was magnificent.
explain.zh: 亨利醒来时很高兴，沉浸在自我满足中，觉得自己表现得很了不起。

q.en: In this book, what does the word 'repulse' mean?
q.zh: 在本书中，单词“repulse”是什么意思？

options.en: To hide behind a large oak tree
options.zh: 躲在一棵大橡树后面

options.en: To sing a short song of victory
options.zh: 唱一首简短的胜利之歌

options.en: To welcome a friend with a happy smile
options.zh: 带着笑容欢迎朋友

options.en: To push back or drive the enemy away
options.zh: 击退或赶走敌人

explain.en: The story says the regiment moved into position 'to repulse', meaning to push the enemy back.
explain.zh: 故事里说队伍就位“to repulse”，意思是把敌人击退。

q.en: When the enemy charges a second time, what does Henry do?
q.zh: 当敌人第二次冲锋时，亨利做了什么？

options.en: He turns and runs toward the rear as fast as he can
options.zh: 他转身拼命朝后方跑去

options.en: He helps a wounded man to a safe place
options.zh: 他帮伤员到了安全的地方

options.en: He bravely stands and leads the line forward according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他勇敢站定，带领队伍前进（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He sits down to rest and eat some food
options.zh: 他坐下来休息并吃东西

explain.en: Seeing others flee, Henry yells in fright and speeds toward the rear, losing his rifle and cap.
explain.zh: 看到别人逃跑，亨利吓得大喊，朝后方飞奔，还弄丢了步枪和帽子。

q.en: In Chapter 7, what does Henry find in the chapel-like woods?
q.zh: 在第7章，亨利在像小教堂一样的树林里发现了什么？

options.en: A dead soldier sitting with his back against a tree
options.zh: 一名死去的士兵背靠大树坐着

options.en: A pile of forgotten army supplies
options.zh: 一堆被遗忘的军需物资

options.en: A small stream where he can drink water
options.zh: 一条他能喝水的小溪

options.en: A friendly squirrel in the tree branches according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 树枝上一只友善的松鼠（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Henry pushes through green doors and sees a dead man seated against a columnlike tree.
explain.zh: 亨利拨开枝叶，看见一名死去的士兵背靠着像柱子一样的树坐着。

q.en: What happens to Henry's rifle and cap during his flight?
q.zh: 亨利逃跑时，他的步枪和帽子怎么了？

options.en: He hides them under a leafy bush
options.zh: 他把它们藏在长叶子的灌木下

options.en: He carefully hands them to a trusted friend for safekeeping
options.zh: 他小心地把它们交给信任的朋友保管

options.en: He loses his rifle and his cap while he runs
options.zh: 他跑的时候丢掉了步枪和帽子

options.en: He trades them for a newer model
options.zh: 他把它们换成了更新的型号

explain.en: The text states his rifle and cap were gone after he turned and ran from the fight.
explain.zh: 原文写道，他转身逃离战斗后，步枪和帽子都不见了。

q.en: How does Henry explain his running away to himself?
q.zh: 亨利是如何向自己解释逃跑行为的？

options.en: He insists a senior officer secretly ordered him to retreat at once
options.zh: 他坚称一名高级军官秘密命令他立刻撤退

options.en: He says he ran to save himself because death was near
options.zh: 他说自己是为了保命而跑，因为死亡逼近

options.en: He explains he was chasing a horse that had run off
options.zh: 他解释说在追一匹跑掉的马

options.en: He claims he went to find water for the whole camp
options.zh: 他声称是去给全营找水

explain.en: Henry tells himself he fled to save his own life because annihilation was approaching.
explain.zh: 亨利对自己说，他逃跑是为了保命，因为毁灭正在逼近。

q.en: The word 'stolid' in this part describes someone who is—
q.zh: 本文中单词“stolid”形容一个人——

options.en: A person who laughs very loudly at everything
options.zh: 对什么都大声大笑的人

options.en: A person who writes long letters home each day
options.zh: 每天写长信回家的人

options.en: A person who runs faster than all the others
options.zh: 比别人都跑得快的人

options.en: A person who shows little or no feeling
options.zh: 几乎不表露感情的人

explain.en: The glossary defines stolid as having or showing little or no emotion.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 stolid 解释为“几乎没有或完全不表露感情的”。

q.en: Why does the author describe nature, like the squirrel and the woods, after Henry flees?
q.zh: 为什么作者在亨利逃跑后描写自然，比如松鼠和树林？

options.en: To explain how soldiers planted trees near the battlefield
options.zh: 解释士兵如何在战场附近种树

options.en: To show nature feels peaceful and helps Henry believe running was natural
options.zh: 表现自然宁静，并让亨利相信逃跑是顺理成章的

options.en: To describe pretty flowers so young readers enjoy the quiet forest according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 描写漂亮的花，让小读者喜欢安静的森林（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To list the animals that live in that part of the country
options.zh: 罗列那个地区生活的动物

explain.en: Henry sees the squirrel flee danger and feels Nature agrees with his choice to run.
explain.zh: 亨利看到松鼠遇险就逃，觉得大自然也认同他逃跑的选择。

q.en: What is the Focus Question for Part 5?
q.zh: 第5部分的“焦点问题”是什么？

options.en: It asks about Henry's inner conflict in this part
options.zh: 它问的是亨利在这一部分的内心冲突

options.en: It asks why the general was riding a small mouse-colored horse
options.zh: 它问将军为什么骑一匹灰鼠色的小马

options.en: It asks where the dead soldier's family lives
options.zh: 它问死去的士兵家人住在哪里

options.en: It asks how many bullets were fired in total
options.zh: 它问一共发射了多少发子弹

explain.en: The Focus Question printed in the book is: 'What inner conflict does Henry experience in this part?'
explain.zh: 书里印的焦点问题是：“在这一部分，亨利经历了怎样的内心冲突？”

prompt.en: Henry runs from the battle but later feels both guilty and proud. What do you think makes a person brave, and can someone be brave even after feeling afraid?
prompt.zh: 亨利从战场逃跑了，但后来既感到内疚又感到骄傲。你认为是什么让一个人勇敢？一个人在害怕之后还能算勇敢吗？

en: Think about a time you felt scared but did something anyway.
Bravery can mean acting even when you are frightened, not never being afraid.
Henry later changes how he sees his own actions—consider why that matters.
zh: 想想你曾经害怕却仍然去做了某件事的时刻。
勇敢可以是“即使害怕仍然行动”，而不是“永远不害怕”。
亨利后来改变了对自己行为的看法——想想这为什么重要。
